name: BBQ Sauce, Adapted from Whole Chile Pepper Book
recipeIngredient: 6 Dried red New Mexican chiles, stemmed
4 Dried tepin or pequín chiles, stemmed and crushed (remove seeds if you don't like much heat)
1 tablespoon Chili powder OR 2 teaspoons Paprika and 1/2 teaspoon Cumin
2 tablespoons Bacon fat or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the chiles with hot water and let soften for 15 minutes.
2. Put them in a blender and whirl until smooth, using about 1 cup of the hot water.
3. In a saucepan, sauté the onion and garlic in the fat until softened.
4. Add the blended chiles and all the remaining ingredients to the saucepan.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil and then lower the heat to low.
6. Simmer for 30 minutes.
7. Cool the sauce, then blend again until smooth.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
